data.title: 7-Eleven plans a major store remodel push in the U.S.
data.description:
7-Eleven plans to remodel at least 7K convenience stores in North America through 2030 as part of a sweeping effort to modernize its network, elevate the in-store experience, and lift sales productivity. The chain, owned by Japan-based Seven & i Holdings Co. (SVNDY), has an ongoing initiative to set a new standard with a large-format, food-centric prototype.

The remodels will apply the 7-Eleven chain's new standard design, first rolled out in late 2024, which features a larger footprint, expanded fresh and prepared food, and an updated layout that supports longer shopping missions rather than quick in-and-out trips only. Notably, modernized stores see about 30% higher traffic in year one and are expected to deliver roughly 44% higher sales after four years versus legacy boxes, giving  the company clear financial rationale for accelerated capital deployment.

Until now, the new standard concept had been primarily tied to new store openings, with a plan for about 1,300 such locations by 2030, but strong performance has pushed the company to scale by retrofitting older units instead of relying solely on new builds.

The remodel push runs concurrent with a major shift in the 7-Eleven ownership model, with around 2,600 company-operated sites planned to be converted to franchised locations by 2030, or roughly 473 conversions annually, more than double the 237 conversions completed in 2025. Seven & i (SVNDY) has emphasized that franchised stores generally generate better financial outcomes with lower operating and administrative costs.

7-Eleven will also reportedly be closing hundreds of stores in North America as part of the broad turnaround plan. Parent company Seven & i Holdings Co. (SVNDY) said 645 convenience stores are slated to close in FY26, which runs through Feb. 28, 2027.

data.title: Boyd Gaming is downgraded by CBRE due to a lack of near-term catalysts
data.description:
CBRE Equity Research downgraded Boyd Gaming (BYD) to a Hold rating after having the casino stock set with a Buy rating.Analyst John DeCree and his team recommended that investors take profit after the recent rally to a new all-time high.DeCree highlighted that Boyd Gaming's (BYD) Q1 results were right down the fairway, with EBITDA of $317M matching the consensus estimate, but pointed to mixed segment results, including Las Vegas Locals EBITDAR down 6.2% year over year and a significant weather benefit for the Midwest and South segment."We see little downside risk at these valuation levels (7.0x FY27 EBITDA); however, we don’t see a near-term catalyst as the lingering destination softness in Las Vegas offsets moderate growth in the rest of BYD’s portfolio. The company has several long-term growth initiatives underway, but these projects won’t begin contributing meaningfully until 2028," updated DeCree.CBRE clipped its price target on Boyd Gaming (BYD) to $100 from $105 to represent a 7.5M trading multiple to the FY27 EBITDA estimate.

Shares of Boyd Gaming (BYD) were down 5.9% on Friday following the release of the Q1 earnings report.

data.title: Eli Lilly to buy Ajax Therapeutics for up to $2.3B in cash
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) has agreed to acquire biopharmaceutical company Ajax Therapeutics for up to $2.3B in cash, inclusive of an upfront payment and subsequent payments upon the achievement of certain clinical and regulatory milestones.

The acquisition builds on Lilly's established capabilities in blood cancers.

Ajax is developing next-generation therapies for blood cancers called myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs), including myelofibrosis. The company’s lead program, AJ1-11095, is a first-in-class Type II JAK2 inhibitor currently in Phase 1 clinical development, with first proof-of-concept clinical data to be presented later in 2026.

The drug has the potential to deliver deeper and more durable disease control than approved therapies for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera.

"As a founding strategic investor in Ajax, Lilly has long believed in the approach and is excited about the potential for AJ1-11095 to deliver deeper and more durable efficacy than available treatments with a tolerability profile that would allow for patients to remain on therapy longer and be used across both the first- and second-line settings," said Jacob Van Naarden, executive vice president and president of Lilly Oncology and head of corporate business development.

data.title: ON Semiconductor, Nio expand pact to accelerate 900V EV platform rollout
data.description:
• ON Semiconductor (ON) on Monday said it is expanding its collaboration with Nio (NIO) for the automaker's shift to higher-voltage EV architectures for improving range and charging speed.
  • The chipmaker said the partnership builds on a multi-year relationship and will help NIO transition from 400-volt to 900-volt vehicle architectures using onsemi’s latest silicon carbide power technology.
  • Higher-voltage systems can improve drivetrain efficiency, enable faster charging, and reduce energy losses, helping extend driving range and boost performance. ON Semi said its technology is expected to support NIO’s latest flagship SUV, the ES9, as well as additional models set to debut at the 2026 Beijing Auto Show.
  • “Electrification is entering a new phase where system efficiency and scalability are paramount,” said Hassane El-Khoury, President and CEO of onsemi. “Our expanded collaboration with NIO demonstrates how deep engineering alignment and aligned technology roadmaps can accelerate the transition to high-voltage architectures."
  • Nio (NIO) shares were up 2.4% premarket.

data.title: Vertiv buys Strategic Thermal Labs
data.description:
• Vertiv (VRT) has acquired Strategic Thermal Labs (STL), a specialist in advanced liquid-cooling technologies.
  • Terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • The acquisition extends Vertiv's thermal-chain strategy by strengthening engineering capability at the interface between server-side liquid cooling and supporting infrastructure.
  • "As AI and high‑performance computing push power densities to unprecedented levels, understanding and solving heat challenges at the chip level becomes critical to system design, performance and reliability," said Scott Armul, chief product and technology officer at Vertiv. "STL brings deep expertise and proven capability in addressing some of the industry's most demanding chip-level density and thermal problems, strengthening Vertiv's ability to emulate and validate system-level solutions and enabling customers to improve performance and lifecycle outcomes in liquid-cooled environments."

data.title: Domino's Pizza falls after same-store sales miss expectations
data.description:
Domino's Pizza (DPZ) reported revenue was up 3.6% year over year to $1.15B, missing the consensus estimate by $10M. International stores saw growth of 4.0%, while U.S. stores generated 2.8% growth. Same-store sales were 1.5% higher at U.S. company-owned stores and 0.8% higher for franchised locations. While both those marks missed the consensus estimate of analysts, the pizza chain noted that Q1 was another quarter of positive order count and market share growth for Domino's in the U.S.

Earnings per share fell 4.6% year over year to $4.13 in Q1 vs. the consensus estimate of $4.27.

A decrease in free cash flow during the quarter was a result of the negative impact of changes in operating assets and liabilities, partially offset by the increase in income from operations 

"In an intensifying macro and competitive environment, our scale advantage and best-in-class store-level profitability uniquely position Domino's in the QSR pizza category to sustain the value and innovation customers demand," highlighted CEO Russ Weiner. "My belief that we can continue to outperform our competition and take meaningful share in 2026 and beyond remains as strong as it has ever been. This is how we will deliver long-term value for our franchisees and shareholders," he added.

Shares of Domino's Pizza (DPZ) were down 4.2% in premarket action to $352.51 vs. the 52-week range of $346.31 to $499.08. Short interest on DPZ stands at 10.2% of the total float.

data.title: Marti Technologies rises 5% on $2.5M buyback
data.description:
• Marti Technologies (MRT) announced a new share‑repurchase program under which the company may buy back up to $2.5M of its outstanding Class A ordinary shares, effective immediately and running until October 26, 2026.
  • In addition, the Board established a ceiling price of $6.00 per share for the share repurchases. As of market close on April 24, 2026, the company's share price was $2.11.
  • The stock price traded 5% higher on Monday during pre-market trade. The company holds a market cap of ~$181M.

data.title: Nestlé S.A. unloads Blue Bottle as it simplifies its coffee portfolio
data.description:
Nestlé S.A. (NSRGY) formally agreed to sell Blue Bottle Coffee to Centurium Capital, which is a Chinese private-equity firm tied to Luckin Coffee (LKNCY). The Swiss food giant's strategic aim with the move is to simplify its portfolio and focus investment behind larger global platforms. The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026, subject to customary conditions, and financial terms were not disclosed.

The deal includes Blue Bottle’s cafés and most of its consumer packaged-goods business, while Nestlé (NSRGY) will retain rights to Blue Bottle-branded single-serve Nespresso pods.

The divestiture underscores Nestlé’s (NSRGY) sharpened focus on scalable, higher-priority businesses rather than operating a global café chain. Of note, the move does not signal a retreat from coffee, as Nestlé (NSRGY) highlighted that coffee was its primary growth driver in the first quarter, led by broad-based momentum in Nescafé. 

Blue Bottle was founded in Oakland, California, in 2002 by James Freeman, a former freelance clarinetist who began roasting coffee in a 186-square-foot converted potting shed. The company became one of the best-known third-wave specialty coffee brands, expanding from the Bay Area to New York, Los Angeles, Tokyo, and other markets before Nestlé (NSRGY) bought a 68% stake in 2017 for $425M to value the business near $700M.

Under Centurium, Blue Bottle is expected to operate independently from Luckin Coffee (LKNCY). Meanwhile, analysts noted that the deal gives Nestlé (NSRGY) a cleaner coffee portfolio centered on Nescafé, Nespresso, and other scaled retail platforms.

data.title: Easterly Government FFO of $0.77 in-line, revenue of $91.55M beats by $3.28M
data.description:

data.title: Ligand Pharmaceuticals to buy XOMA Royalty in $740M all-cash deal: WSJ
data.description:
• Ligand Pharmaceuticals (LGND) has reportedly reached a $740M all-cash deal to acquire XOMA Royalty (XOMA), the Wall Street Journal reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The deal would value Xoma at $39 a share, the people said, with a deal likely to be announced as soon as Monday. Xoma had a market value of around $450M as of Friday, or close to $700M on a fully diluted basis, according to FactSet.
  • By absorbing Xoma, Ligand’s portfolio would contain more than 200 drugs and experimental treatments, the report said.
  • XOMA shares were up 2.9% premarket to $39.
